items.headline: Middle East Escalation Intensifies
items.text: Israel launched wide-scale strikes on IRGC headquarters in Tehran. Saudi Arabia expelled Iranian military staff. Trump claims 15 points of agreement, but Iran denies any dialogue. Five-day pause on energy site strikes, but Hormuz shipping lanes remain under threat.

items.headline: AI Coding Wars Heat Up
items.text: Claude Opus 4.6 leads SWE-Bench Verified at 80.8%, ahead of GPT-5.4's 77.2%. OpenAI's GPT-5.4 ships with 1.05M token context. Chinese models from MiniMax and Xiaomi match Western quality at dramatically lower cost. The race is no longer about who is smartest. It is about who is cheapest.

items.headline: Crypto Relief Rally Amid Extreme Fear
items.text: Bitcoin reclaimed $71K with a 3.79% surge. Ethereum outperformed at +5.16%. The Fear and Greed Index sits at 11, the deepest extreme fear reading this year. Equities rallied too. The market is betting on containment. If that changes, crypto goes first.

items.headline: Summary
items.text: Israel hit Tehran. Bitcoin rallied. Claude writes better code than most developers. That is your world on March 24, 2026.

items.headline: AI
items.text: Claude Opus 4.6 now scores 80.8% on SWE-Bench Verified, pulling ahead of GPT-5.4. OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 with a 1.05 million token context window. China's MiniMax M2.5 matches Claude quality at a fraction of the cost. The AI coding war is real, and your IDE is the battlefield.

items.headline: World
items.text: Israel struck IRGC headquarters in Tehran in a wide-scale wave of attacks. Iran says there are no talks with Washington despite Trump claiming 15 points of agreement. Saudi Arabia expelled Iranian diplomats. The Strait of Hormuz remains the world's pressure point.

items.headline: Markets
items.text: S&P 500 up 1.15%. NASDAQ up 1.38%. Bitcoin reclaimed $71K on a relief rally despite extreme fear at 11. Ethereum outperformed at +5.16%. The market is pricing in a short war. If that bet is wrong, expect fireworks.

items.headline: Wild Card
items.text: Mistral is quietly sprinting to $1B ARR while everyone watches the OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic race. Enterprise customers do not want the biggest model. They want the cheapest one that works. China knows this. Mistral knows this. Does Silicon Valley?

items.headline: GPT-5.4 Launches with 1.05M Token Context
items.text: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 on March 5, designed for professional multi-step tasks. The context window is the largest OpenAI has ever offered commercially. Factual accuracy improved 33% on individual claims. Ties with Gemini 3.1 Pro at 57 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index.

items.headline: Claude Opus 4.6 Leads SWE-Bench at 80.8%
items.text: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 scores 80.8% on SWE-Bench Verified, ahead of GPT-5.4's 77.2%. The 1M token context window is now in beta across both Opus and Sonnet tiers, allowing entire codebases in a single conversation. Depth over breadth continues to be Anthropic's strategy.

items.headline: Gemini 3.1 Pro Hits 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2
items.text: Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.1 Pro features a 1M token context window with multimodal reasoning across text, images, audio, video, and code. It leads the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at 57 points, tied with GPT-5.4 Pro. The most capable Pro-tier model as of February 2026.

items.headline: Chinese AI Models Match Western Quality
items.text: Five new models dropped from Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance, and MiniMax this month. MiniMax's M2.5 rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at significantly lower cost. Xiaomi's MiMo-V2-Pro, led by a former DeepSeek researcher, is turning heads. China is winning the cost efficiency race.

items.headline: Kimi K2.5 Optimized for Agentic Tasks
items.text: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 is now available in Cloudflare Workers AI with 256K token context. Built specifically for multi-step agentic tasks, tool calling, and visual understanding. The model is optimized for workflows where AI agents plan, act, and iterate without human intervention.

items.headline: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super: 120B for Multi-Agent
items.text: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Super, a 120 billion parameter model designed specifically for multi-agent applications. Purpose-built for orchestrating multiple AI agents working together on complex tasks. Signals that the hardware giant is moving deeper into the model layer.

items.headline: MIT Protein-Based Drug Design Model
items.text: MIT researchers released a model that predicts how synthetic proteins fold and interact. This could reduce pharmaceutical R&D costs by billions and accelerate treatment for cancer, autoimmune diseases, and rare genetic disorders. Medical AI is moving from diagnosis to drug design.

items.headline: Mistral Forge: Build-Your-Own Enterprise AI
items.text: Mistral launched Forge at NVIDIA GTC, a platform letting enterprises build custom AI models without sharing proprietary data. The company is on track to surpass $1B ARR this year. Their laser focus on enterprise is working while competitors chase consumer market share.

items.headline: Google Stitch Disrupts Design Tools
items.text: Google launched Stitch, a free AI design tool that builds high-fidelity reports from plain language. It caused Figma's stock to drop 12% on launch day. The tool represents the next wave: AI that does not assist designers but replaces the design step entirely for standard outputs.

items.headline: Pentagon Labels Anthropic a Supply-Chain Risk
items.text: The DoD labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the company refused to allow its technology for mass surveillance or autonomous weapons. Over 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees filed statements in Anthropic's defense. The White House released a six-part AI legislative blueprint.

items.headline: Agentic Workflows Become Dominant Productivity Trend
items.text: Gartner predicts over 40% of enterprise apps will embed role-specific AI agents by end of 2026. No-code platforms like Gumloop, n8n, and Zapier Central let anyone build agent workflows. Tech leader Andrej Karpathy notes software engineering is now 80% delegation to AI agents.

items.headline: OpenAI Superapp Consolidation
items.text: OpenAI consolidated ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into a single desktop Superapp, the largest UX consolidation in AI history. Complete with a 1.05M token context window. The strategy: one app to rule them all. Mirrors the WeChat playbook: everything in one place, no switching.

items.headline: Israel Strikes IRGC Headquarters in Tehran
items.text: The Israeli Air Force carried out a wide-scale wave of strikes on Iranian regime infrastructure, hitting one of the IRGC's main headquarters and several military buildings in Tehran. The strikes represent a significant escalation in the direct confrontation between Israel and Iran.

items.headline: Trump Claims 15 Points of Agreement with Iran
items.text: President Trump announced 15 points of agreement with Iran and stated he will hold off strikes against Iranian energy sites for five days. However, Iran's foreign ministry denied any dialogue between Tehran and Washington. The gap between American claims and Iranian statements grows wider.

items.headline: Israel Destroys All Crossings Over Litani River
items.text: In Lebanon, Israel struck the main bridge linking the south to the rest of the country. The Israeli military ordered its troops to destroy all crossings over the Litani River and step up demolition of homes near the southern border. Southern Lebanon is being systematically isolated.

items.headline: Saudi Arabia Expels Iranian Diplomats
items.text: Saudi Arabia ordered Iran's military attache and four embassy staff to leave the kingdom within 24 hours. The Saudi Foreign Ministry cited repeated Iranian attacks on Saudi territory. This reverses the China-brokered rapprochement of 2023 and signals a deeper regional fracture.

items.headline: 64 Killed in Sudan Hospital Strike
items.text: At least 64 people were killed in a strike on a hospital in East Darfur, with 89 injured according to the WHO. Sudan's civil war continues with devastating humanitarian consequences. Medical facilities remain targets despite international law protections.

items.headline: Cuba Partially Restores Power After Grid Collapse
items.text: Cuban authorities partially restored electricity after more than 10 million people lost power following another collapse of the national power grid. This is the latest in a series of grid failures hitting the island. The aging infrastructure cannot keep up with demand.

items.headline: White House Releases AI Legislative Blueprint
items.text: The White House released a six-part AI legislative blueprint addressing safety, competition, and national security. Palantir's Maven AI was designated as a formal program of record with long-term Pentagon funding. The US government is moving from AI strategy to AI implementation.

items.headline: Hormuz Strait Tensions Keep Oil Elevated
items.text: Oil prices remain elevated as Strait of Hormuz shipping lanes face continued threat from the Iran-Israel conflict. Any disruption to the strait, through which 20% of global oil flows, would send energy prices spiking. Markets watch every naval movement in the region.

items.headline: Oil (Brent)
items.text: $110+ (Elevated)

items.headline: Sentiment
items.text: 11 (Extreme Fear)

items.headline: BuildFast with AI
items.text: 12+ AI Models in March 2026: The Week That Changed AI | GPT-5.4 Pro: OpenAI's new flagship ties with Gemini 3.1 Pro at 57 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. The 1.05M token context window is the largest commercially available. Designed for complex multi-step professional tasks. Claude Opus 4.6: Anthropic's depth-first strategy pays off with 80.8% on SWE-Bench Verified. The 1M context beta means entire codebases fit in one conversation. This is the model professional developers are switching to. MiniMax M2.5: The standout Chinese model that rivals Claude at dramatically lower cost. This is the price disruption that Western labs feared. Enterprise buyers are paying attention. OpenAI Superapp consolidation: ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas merged into one desktop app. This is the biggest UX change in AI history. One interface for chat, code, and research. The WeChat playbook comes to AI. NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super: A 120B parameter model built for multi-agent orchestration. NVIDIA is not just selling GPUs anymore. They are building the software layer that makes those GPUs useful for agent swarms. Kimi K2.5 from Moonshot AI now in Cloudflare Workers with 256K context for agentic tasks Xiaomi's MiMo-V2-Pro built by former DeepSeek researcher Luo Fuli WordPress adds AI integrations letting agents draft posts and manage content "The differences between GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Claude 4.6 on most practical tasks are increasingly marginal. The real battle is cost, speed, and ecosystem lock-in."

items.headline: Deloitte Insights
items.text: Agentic AI Strategy: From Hype to Enterprise Reality | 40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by end of 2026 according to Gartner predictions. The shift from chatbots to autonomous agents is accelerating faster than anyone expected. Four architecture patterns dominate: single agent, hierarchical multi-agent, sequential pipeline, and decentralized swarm. The key is matching architecture to use case, not chasing maximum autonomy. Why agentic implementations fail: Most organizations try to automate existing processes instead of reimagining operations. Leading companies manage AI agents as workers with clear roles, KPIs, and escalation paths. The winners treat agents like new hires, not software. No-code agent builders: Gumloop, n8n, Zapier Central, and Make.com now offer visual agent builders. For simple to intermediate workflows, you do not need coding skills. The barrier to building your first autonomous workflow has never been lower. Gumloop: Visual agent builder for non-technical teams n8n: Open-source workflow automation with AI agent nodes Claude Code: CLI-based agentic coding with 1M context "Agentic AI is no longer a research concept. They are the most transformative productivity tool since the spreadsheet."

items.headline: RiskInfo AI
items.text: AI Insights: Key Global Developments in March 2026 | Pentagon designates Palantir's Maven AI as a formal program of record with long-term funding. AI integration in government is accelerating from strategy documents to operational deployment. MIT protein drug design: A new model predicts synthetic protein folding, potentially cutting pharma R&D costs by billions. Cancer, autoimmune, and rare disease treatments could arrive years faster. AI moving from theory to practice: The bigger story of March 2026 is not new model releases. It is how AI is being embedded into real-world systems, government programs, military operations, and medical research. The gap between AI capability and AI deployment is closing rapidly. "The period from late February into mid-March saw AI moving from theory into practice. New model releases grab headlines but the bigger story is how AI is embedded into real-world systems."

items.headline: OpenAI
items.text: GPT-5.4 launched with 1.05M context. Superapp consolidation merges ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into one desktop app. Ties with Gemini at 57 on the Intelligence Index. 33% fewer factual errors. Going all-in on the single-app strategy.

items.headline: Google DeepMind
items.text: Gemini 3.1 Pro leads the Intelligence Index. 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 with full multimodal reasoning. Google Stitch launched as a free AI design tool, causing Figma to drop 12%. DeepMind is attacking the creative tools market while the model race tightens.

items.headline: Anthropic
items.text: Claude Opus 4.6 leads SWE-Bench at 80.8%. 1M context in beta. Over 30 OpenAI and Google employees filed statements defending Anthropic after the Pentagon labeled it a supply-chain risk for refusing to enable mass surveillance. Integrity as a differentiator.

items.headline: Meta AI
items.text: Meta continues investing heavily in open-source AI models. Llama family remains the go-to for self-hosted enterprise deployments. The open-source vs closed-source battle intensifies as Chinese models prove you can match top performance at fraction of the cost.

items.headline: Mistral
items.text: Launched Forge at NVIDIA GTC for enterprise custom model building. On track to surpass $1B ARR this year. Laser focus on enterprise is working. While big labs chase benchmarks, Mistral quietly captures the customers who pay. The European dark horse is galloping.

items.headline: China Challengers
items.text: Five models from Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance, and MiniMax in one month. MiniMax M2.5 matches Claude at lower cost. Xiaomi's MiMo-V2-Pro led by ex-DeepSeek talent. The cost efficiency race is theirs to lose. Western labs need a pricing response.

items.headline: Avoid the AI Exoskeleton Effect
items.text: If you let AI do all the thinking, you lose the ability to think. Use AI as a force multiplier, not a replacement for your judgment. Review every output. Question the reasoning. The moment you stop checking is the moment you become dependent on a system you do not understand.

items.headline: Build Agentic Workflows Without Code
items.text: Gumloop, n8n, and Zapier Central now let you build autonomous AI workflows with drag-and-drop interfaces. Connect an AI agent to your email, CRM, and calendar. Let it triage, draft, and schedule. Start with one simple workflow and expand from there. No engineering team required.

items.headline: NVIDIA Nemotron for Multi-Agent Projects
items.text: If you are building systems where multiple AI agents need to coordinate, NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Super (120B) is purpose-built for this. It handles agent orchestration natively. Useful for teams building customer service, research, or operations automation with multiple specialized agents.

items.headline: Google Stitch for Instant Design
items.text: Google's new Stitch tool generates high-fidelity UI designs from plain language descriptions. It is free and it caused Figma to drop 12% on launch day. If you need mockups, reports, or interface designs and do not have a designer, this is your shortcut. Skip the learning curve entirely.

items.headline: Daily AI Habit: Use, Review, Learn
items.text: HubSpot's Dharmesh Shah recommends a three-part strategy for keeping up: use AI daily in your actual work, schedule quarterly reviews of new tools, and try one new tool per week with a specific problem in mind. Consistency beats intensity. Small daily experiments compound.

items.headline: Best Coding Model Right Now: Claude Opus 4.6
items.text: For coding tasks specifically, Claude Opus 4.6 with its 1M context window and 80.8% SWE-Bench score is the current leader. Use Claude Code for CLI-based agentic coding. Pair it with Cursor IDE for an integrated experience. The model that understands your entire codebase wins.

items.headline: GPT-5.4 Goes Live: 1M Tokens, Autonomous Workflows
items.text: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 is not just a better chatbot. It can read your entire codebase, your full contract history, your year of emails, all at once, and take action across software environments autonomously. The 1.05M-token context window is now the commercial standard. If you haven't tested it on a real work task yet, today is the day. Release notes ↗

items.headline: Hormuz Crisis: Oil at $111 and Climbing
items.text: The Strait of Hormuz handled 20 million barrels per day before the war. It's now near zero. Brent crude is up 50% this month. Trump paused US strikes for 5 days to negotiate, but nothing is resolved. This is not a short-term spike. Plan your energy exposure and supply chain accordingly. CNN Live Updates ↗

items.headline: Google Stitch: Free AI Design Tool Disrupts Professional Work
items.text: Google Stitch turned a napkin sketch into a board-ready CFO report with one prompt. It's free. It critiques its own work in real time. Figma lost 12% of its stock value the day it launched. If you're still manually building financial presentations, stop. Test Stitch today. finstory AI ↗

items.headline: Summary
items.text: The world is on fire, oil is at $111, and AI just got a 1-million-token brain. Pick which one you care about more. Both will reshape how you work this week.

items.headline: AI
items.text: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.4 with a 1.05 million token context window and the ability to autonomously execute multi-step workflows. That is not an upgrade. That is a category shift. At the same time, Google Stitch dropped free and Figma stock fell 12% on day one. The pattern is clear: AI is making professional tools free. Finance reporting is next.

items.headline: World
items.text: Day 23 of the US-Israel-Iran war. Iran struck Arad and Dimona, injuring 100+. Trump threatened to obliterate Iran's power plants. Then he backed off for 5 days, citing "productive conversations." The Strait of Hormuz is still disrupted. IEA says this is the largest oil supply shock in history. Cuba went dark for the third time this month. Sudan lost 64 civilians in a hospital strike.

items.headline: Markets
items.text: Everything is red. S&P 500 down 1.51%. NASDAQ down 1.29%. BTC at $68k, ETH at $2,062. The one thing going up: oil. Brent crude at $111, up over 50% this month alone. Hormuz flows dropped from 20 million barrels per day to nearly zero. Goldman Sachs says triple-digit oil could stick for years. Energy sector is the only hedge that's working right now.

items.headline: Wild Card
items.text: Chinese AI is not losing. Five Chinese models dropped this month. MiniMax M2.5 now rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at a fraction of the cost. Kimi K2.5 runs on Cloudflare's edge. The West is spending billions on AI infrastructure. China is shipping cheaper, faster models. The cost war is on. And the winner is every developer who does not care which flag is on the model card.

items.headline: GPT-5.4: 1.05M Tokens, Autonomous Execution, 75% on OSWorld-V
items.text: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 sets a new commercial benchmark with a 1.05-million-token context window. The model scores 75% on OSWorld-V, just above the human baseline of 72.4%, and can autonomously execute multi-step workflows across software environments. Mini version now rolling out to Free and Go users. OpenAI has surpassed $25B in annualized revenue and is eyeing a late-2026 IPO.

items.headline: Gemini 3.1 Pro: 77.1% ARC-AGI-2, 1M Context, Full Multimodal
items.text: Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.1 Pro scores 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 with a 1M-token context window and multimodal reasoning across text, images, audio, video, and code. Flash-Lite variant offers 2.5x faster responses at just $0.25 per million input tokens, making it the most cost-effective frontier model available.

items.headline: Google Stitch: Free AI Design Tool Crashes Figma 12%
items.text: Google Stitch builds working, high-fidelity reports and interfaces from plain-language prompts. No wireframes, no design background needed. It critiques its own work in real time, responds to voice, and deploys in one click. The day it launched, Figma dropped 12%. Finance reporting, legal documents, and board materials are all in the crosshairs next.

items.headline: Five Chinese Models Drop: MiniMax M2.5 Rivals Claude Opus 4.6
items.text: Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance, and MiniMax all launched models in March. MiniMax's M2.5 rivals Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 at a significantly lower cost. Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 is now available in Cloudflare Workers AI for edge inference, no server management required. Chinese models are winning the cost war.

items.headline: Nemotron 3 Super: 120B Parameters, 12B Active, Built for Agents
items.text: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Super is a 120B-total-parameter hybrid Mixture-of-Experts model with only 12B active parameters per forward pass. Announced at GTC on March 11, it's designed specifically for complex multi-agent enterprise applications where inference efficiency matters as much as raw capability.

items.headline: Anthropic Rolls Out Memory for All Claude Users
items.text: Anthropic's persistent memory feature is now available to all Claude users. The assistant retains context, preferences, and project history across conversations. Combined with Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 (released in February), Anthropic now offers a 1-million-token context window in beta with improved coding capabilities. Over 30 OpenAI and Google employees publicly backed Anthropic's DOD lawsuit.

items.headline: MIT AI Model Slashes Drug Development Costs by Billions
items.text: MIT's protein-based drug design model predicts how synthetic proteins fold and interact with molecular targets, cutting pharmaceutical R&D costs by billions of dollars. The model accelerates treatment development for cancer, autoimmune diseases, and rare genetic disorders. Medical AI matured from research curiosity to pipeline reality this month.

items.headline: Kimi K2.5 on Cloudflare Edge: Frontier AI Without Servers
items.text: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 is now available in the Cloudflare Workers AI ecosystem, enabling developers to run a frontier-level AI model directly on edge computing infrastructure. No servers to manage, no GPU clusters to provision. This signals the next phase: frontier-grade AI as a commodity infrastructure layer.

items.headline: Iran-US-Israel War Day 23: Hormuz Ultimatum, Trump Pauses Strikes
items.text: Iran struck the Israeli cities of Arad and Dimona (near Israel's nuclear research center), injuring 100+ people. Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on Iran's largest power plant. Iran warned it would "completely close" Hormuz if power plants are struck. Trump then halted all US energy infrastructure strikes for five days, citing productive diplomatic conversations. Nothing is resolved.

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz: IEA Calls It the Worst Supply Shock in History
items.text: The International Energy Agency warns the Hormuz disruption is the largest supply shock in the history of the global oil market. Crude and oil product flows through the Strait have fallen from around 20 million barrels per day before the war to near zero currently. Goldman Sachs says triple-digit oil prices could persist for years if the conflict is not resolved.

items.headline: Ukraine: Low-Tech Overhead Netting Deployed Against Russian FPV Drones
items.text: Ukrainian towns are installing overhead netting as a low-cost defense against Russian FPV (first-person view) drones — the latest generation of cheap, precise, lethal weapons deployed by Russian forces. Cities along the advancing eastern front line are preparing infrastructure for sustained urban combat. Russia's full-scale invasion is now in its fifth year.

items.headline: Cuba Goes Dark for Third Time in March as Grid Collapses Again
items.text: Cuba's power grid collapsed for the third time in March, leaving the entire country without electricity. The communist government is battling a combination of decaying Soviet-era infrastructure, lack of investment, and a US oil blockade that has gutted energy generation capacity. The recurring blackouts signal a systemic collapse, not a temporary failure.

items.headline: Sentiment
items.text: Fear (Hormuz Risk)

items.headline: finstory AI
items.text: I turned a napkin sketch into a board-ready CFO report with AI (one prompt) | Google Stitch is free and it's already breaking things: Google launched Stitch this week — a free AI design tool that builds working, board-quality reports and interfaces from plain-language prompts. No wireframes. No Figma. No design agency. The author tested it on real financial data: uploaded a photo of hand-drawn numbers, typed one prompt, and got back a premium executive financial report complete with a clean headline, 12-month trend charts with green/red performance indicators, a comparison table, and three FP&A driver callouts. Output quality equivalent to three hours of analyst work on a Sunday night. Figma lost 12% the day Stitch launched: That market signal is the story. Google just made free what professional teams used to pay $50-100 per seat per month for. This pattern does not stay in design software. It moves to every category of professional work. Finance reporting is next. Legal document generation after that. The prompt is the FP&A judgment: The key insight in this issue is not about the tool. It's about how you prompt it. "Don't ask for a design. Ask for FP&A judgment built into a visual format." That is a completely different instruction. Generic AI outputs look generic because the prompt was generic. Specificity in the prompt produces specificity in the output. The author provides a copy-paste prompt template for generating CFO-ready reports. The professional work disruption curve is accelerating: Stitch is a signal, not an endpoint. AI is systematically making professional tools free. Each disruption follows the same pattern: tool launches free, incumbents drop in market value, early adopters get an unfair advantage for 6-12 months before the skill becomes table stakes. You are inside the 6-12 month window right now for finance AI tools. You can see the live demo at google-stitch-finstory.netlify.app Stitch critiques its own work in real time and responds to voice commands One-click deploy to production — no engineering required "The difference between a generic AI output and a boardroom-ready one is how you frame the prompt. Don't ask for a design. Ask for FP&A judgment built into a visual format."

items.headline: Pinecone
items.text: February/March Global Newsletter: BYOC on All 3 Clouds, HIPAA Standard, Deletion at Scale | BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) now in public preview on AWS, GCP, and Azure: This is a major enterprise unlock. BYOC lets you run Pinecone's data plane inside your own cloud account. Your vectors, metadata, and queries never leave your environment. Setup is fully self-serve via a Pulumi-based wizard with pull-based operations that execute locally in your cluster. No SSH, no VPN, no inbound network access required. This removes the #1 objection for enterprise AI deployments: data sovereignty. HIPAA compliance for Standard plan at $190/month: Previously available only on Enterprise. Starting now, Standard plan customers can add HIPAA compliance including encrypted data storage, audit logging, enhanced security controls, and BAA execution support. This opens the healthcare AI market to mid-market companies that couldn't afford Enterprise pricing. Garbage Day — How Pinecone Safely Deletes Billions of Objects: Deleting data at scale is harder than adding it. Pinecone's Janitor garbage collection system safely removes billions of objects from immutable blob storage without disrupting live queries. The post covers why deletion in distributed systems requires more than a delete call, and how Pinecone keeps storage costs low without sacrificing query reliability. Essential reading if you're running a production vector database. When "Performance" Means Two Different Things: Speed and accuracy are both called "performance" in AI, but optimizing for one trades off against the other. This post breaks down the distinction for production systems: when you need latency-optimized retrieval vs. when precision matters more. A practical framework for setting the right performance target before you build. Upcoming webinar: Pinecone Platform Series — Slab Architecture (March 24) Upcoming webinar: Nuances of Hybrid Search (March 26) Vector Search for Agentic AI hands-on introduction series starting April 23 Pinecone + n8n official node: The official Pinecone Assistant node for n8n is live, enabling no-code vector search workflows in automated pipelines. 10M-vector benchmark guide: New guide covers how to benchmark Pinecone at 10M+ vectors with real performance data. "BYOC removes the #1 enterprise objection: data sovereignty. Your vectors, metadata, and queries never leave your environment."

items.headline: TLDR
items.text: Meta's CEO Agent, Amazon's AI phone, SpaceX + Tesla chip factory | Meta is building a CEO agent: Mark Zuckerberg is building an AI agent to help him run Meta. He envisions a future where everyone has their own AI agents handling tasks, communication, and decision support. This is not a chatbot experiment. This is the CEO of a $1.5T company betting his own workflow on autonomous AI. Amazon's AI-native phone: Amazon is developing a phone built around AI from the ground up, not AI bolted onto an existing OS. If it ships, it would be the first major consumer hardware designed for agentic workflows by default. SpaceX + Tesla chip factory: Elon Musk's "Terafab" AI chip factory will combine SpaceX and Tesla resources to manufacture custom AI silicon. The goal is to reduce dependency on NVIDIA and build chips optimized for Tesla's self-driving and xAI's Grok infrastructure. 85% of YC's W26 batch is AI-first. 56 of 198 companies are building fully autonomous agents positioned as AI employees. Gemini (crypto exchange) hit with class-action lawsuit over IPO disclosures.

items.headline: The Rundown AI
items.text: Elon Musk's 'Terafab' AI chip factory + Google Stitch website redesign | Terafab is Musk's play for AI hardware independence: The Rundown breaks down how the factory would produce custom chips for both Tesla FSD training and xAI's Grok models. Current NVIDIA dependency is a bottleneck for both companies. Vertical integration is the move. Google Stitch website redesign tutorial: Step-by-step guide on using Google Stitch to completely redesign a website from scratch using only prompts. The tool generates responsive HTML/CSS and deploys in one click. This is the practical how-to the finstory AI newsletter teased.

items.headline: Superhuman AI
items.text: Scientists use plastic to beat Parkinson's | Plastic-based treatment for Parkinson's: Scientists developed a plastic material that can deliver dopamine-producing cells directly to the brain, bypassing the blood-brain barrier. Early results show significant motor function improvement in animal models. This could change the treatment paradigm for neurodegenerative diseases. AI in scientific discovery is accelerating: This week's issue highlights how AI models are now being used to predict protein folding, drug interactions, and treatment outcomes at speeds that compress years of research into weeks. The pattern: AI handles the combinatorial explosion of possibilities, humans handle the judgment calls.

items.headline: simple.ai by Dharmesh
items.text: A 3-Part Strategy for Keeping Up with AI | Use AI daily, do not just study it: Dharmesh Shah (HubSpot co-founder) argues that hands-on experience teaches more than reading research papers. The most important thing is not tracking every development but actually using AI in your day-to-day work. He recommends tools like Company Research Agent, Prospect Extractor, and ICP Builder on agent.ai. Schedule quarterly AI opportunity reviews: Every quarter, take your top 3 growth obstacles and check if new AI tools can now address them. Keep a "not yet, but soon" list. Companies that evaluated AI once and gave up fell behind competitors who revisited regularly. Try new tools with specific problems in mind: Problem-first evaluation beats aimless exploration. When Claude 3.7 Sonnet launched, Dharmesh tested it specifically for code review efficiency and found hybrid reasoning was remarkably effective at understanding complex codebases. "What you shouldn't do is decide at any point that AI is not good enough to tackle a problem and then forget about it. The answer might change faster than you expect."

items.headline: OpenAI
items.text: GPT-5.4 launched with 1.05M token context and autonomous execution (75% OSWorld-V). $25B annualized revenue, eyeing a late-2026 IPO. GPT-5.4 mini rolling out to Free and Go users. Autonomous multi-step software execution marks a shift from assistant to agent.

items.headline: Google DeepMind
items.text: Gemini 3.1 Pro at 77.1% ARC-AGI-2. Flash-Lite at $0.25/M tokens (2.5x faster). Stitch design tool disrupted Figma on day one. Google is attacking incumbents with free AI tools while also competing at the frontier model level.

items.headline: Anthropic
items.text: Memory features now live for all users. Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 released in February with 1M-token context in beta. Over 30 OpenAI and Google employees publicly backed Anthropic in its DOD lawsuit. Cross-industry support signals growing trust in Anthropic's safety-focused approach.

items.headline: Mistral
items.text: Forge enterprise platform launched at NVIDIA GTC, enabling "build-your-own-AI" for enterprises. On track to surpass $1B annual recurring revenue this year. Mistral's bet: enterprises want full control over their AI stack, not dependence on OpenAI or Google.

items.headline: Meta AI
items.text: Llama deployments continue expanding across enterprise. Meta's open-source strategy keeps Llama in every stack where developers want to avoid vendor lock-in. No major model launch this week but Meta's infrastructure bets (custom silicon, data center expansion) keep it competitive on cost.

items.headline: China AI
items.text: Five models launched this month from Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, ByteDance, and MiniMax. MiniMax M2.5 rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at a fraction of the cost. Kimi K2.5 runs on Cloudflare's edge. Chinese AI is winning on price and shipping speed. The West's infrastructure spending advantage may not translate to model quality lead for long.

items.headline: Don't Let AI Become Your Exoskeleton
items.text: GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 can do your thinking for you. That is exactly the problem. Use AI to accelerate your thinking, not replace it. Feed your analysis to the model. Don't ask the model for your analysis. The exoskeleton effect is real: professionals who outsource judgment to AI lose the muscle over time. Keep the hard thinking in your head. Use AI for execution and research.

items.headline: Build Agentic Workflows Without Code
items.text: n8n, Make.com, and Zapier Central all let you build AI agents visually. No code required. A research agent that runs daily costs $5-20/month in API fees. The 2026 architecture that matters most: a RAG-grounded agent with a vector database backend (Pinecone), a frontier model for reasoning (GPT-5.4 or Claude), and a workflow layer for orchestration (n8n). Build this and you have an employee that works 24/7 for $25/month.

items.headline: Turn Napkin Numbers into CFO Reports with Google Stitch
items.text: Google Stitch is free. Go to stitch.google.com, upload a photo of your hand-drawn numbers or paste raw data, and use this prompt template: "Build an executive financial report. Lead with a single headline that states the conclusion. Show three key metrics with context. Include a 12-month trend chart where green = over plan, red = under. Add three driver callouts explaining the story." You will get a board-ready report in under 60 seconds.

items.headline: GPT-5.4 is Now the Best Coding Model for Long Context
items.text: 1.05 million tokens means GPT-5.4 can read your entire codebase in one pass. For refactoring, debugging across files, and architecture reviews, this is the new standard. Use it with a strong system prompt: "You are a senior engineer reviewing this codebase. Identify the three highest-risk areas. For each, explain the risk, the impact, and the fix." You get a full engineering audit in seconds.

items.headline: Use Pinecone + n8n for Persistent AI Memory
items.text: Anthropic added memory to Claude. But if you want cross-platform, cross-model AI memory, build it yourself. Store all your conversations, documents, and work outputs as vector embeddings in Pinecone. Connect it to n8n. Every time you start a new AI conversation, retrieve the top 5 most relevant memories first. This gives you context persistence across any model, any tool, any platform.

items.headline: Deploy Frontier AI on Cloudflare Workers: Zero Servers
items.text: Kimi K2.5 is now available in Cloudflare Workers AI. You can call a frontier-level AI model in a Cloudflare Worker with three lines of code. No GPU servers, no scaling headaches, no cold starts. This is the cheapest way to add AI to any web application. Response times are under 200ms globally. Try it: build a one-page app that summarizes any URL you paste into it. Cost: near zero.

items.headline: Iran-Hormuz Crisis Week 4
items.text: Missiles hit near Dimona nuclear center, Trump gives 48hr ultimatum to reopen Strait. Oil above $100/barrel threatening global energy supply.

items.headline: AI Superapp Wars
items.text: OpenAI consolidating into desktop Superapp, Google Stitch disrupts design (Figma -12%), Claude Opus 4.6 leads SWE-Bench at 80.8%.

items.headline: Market Stress
items.text: S&P down 1.51%, NASDAQ down 2.01%, crypto also falling. Goldman says oil may stay at triple digits for years.

items.headline: Summary
items.text: Three wars are running in parallel right now. One involves missiles near a nuclear reactor. Another is an AI arms race where four companies shipped frontier models in a single week. The third is oil at $106 a barrel dragging every portfolio on earth into the red. Here is the breakdown.

items.headline: AI
items.text: OpenAI merged ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into one desktop Superapp with a 1.05M token context window. Google fired back with Stitch, a free design tool that tanked Figma stock 12% in a single session. Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 now leads every coding benchmark at 80.8% SWE-Bench. The frontier model war just became a platform war. Pick your stack carefully.

items.headline: World
items.text: Iran fired missiles near Israel's Dimona nuclear center. That is a first. 100+ injured, and Trump gave a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Over 5,000 vessels are stranded. Iran responded by threatening full closure. Diego Garcia, a US-UK base in the Indian Ocean, also took fire. This is not contained anymore.

items.headline: Markets
items.text: Everything is red. S&P down 1.51%. NASDAQ down 2.01%. Bitcoin dropped 2.77% to $68,616. Brent crude surged past $106 on Hormuz fears. Goldman says triple-digit oil could last years if the strait stays closed. The sell-off is energy-driven, not tech-driven. Watch oil, not earnings.

items.headline: Wild Card
items.text: The Pentagon just made Palantir's Maven a permanent program of record. That means long-term military AI funding across every branch. AI in defense went from experimental to infrastructure overnight. Meanwhile, you can now run DeepSeek and Llama on your phone with zero internet. Private AI is real. The gap between what governments build and what individuals can run is shrinking fast.

items.headline: OpenAI Superapp
items.text: GPT-5.4 consolidation of ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into a single desktop application. Features 1.05M token context window. Represents the largest UX consolidation in AI history, targeting $25B+ revenue.

items.headline: Google Gemini 3.1 Pro
items.text: Tops the intelligence index at 57 points with 1M context window and 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 score. Full multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video makes it the most versatile model released this quarter.

items.headline: Anthropic Claude 4.6
items.text: Achieves 80.8% on SWE-Bench Verified, leading all coding benchmarks. Pentagon DOD lawsuit draws support from OpenAI and Google employees. Enterprise deployment expanding via Microsoft partnership.

items.headline: Google Stitch
items.text: Free AI design tool that generates high-fidelity reports from natural language prompts. Converts napkin sketches into board-ready executive reports. Figma stock dropped 12% on launch day, signaling a major disruption.

items.headline: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super
items.text: 120B-parameter hybrid mixture-of-experts model with only 12B active per forward pass. Built specifically for multi-agent applications. Announced at GTC 2026 as a cornerstone of NVIDIA's AI software stack.

items.headline: MiniMax M2.5
items.text: Rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at significantly lower cost, emerging as a new challenger in the frontier model race. Strong performance across coding and reasoning benchmarks positions it as a cost-effective alternative.

items.headline: Mistral Forge
items.text: Enterprise platform for building custom AI models. Company on track for $1B ARR with CEO confirming enterprise focus is paying off. GPU-efficient architecture differentiates from larger competitors.

items.headline: Palantir Maven
items.text: Pentagon designates Maven as a formal program of record with long-term military funding across all branches. Marks the transition of AI from experimental defense projects to core military infrastructure.

items.headline: Cursor Plan Mode
items.text: Users commit at 2x the rate with Plan Mode enabled. Head of Design shows designers coding actual features instead of Figma mockups. Shift+Tab activates structured step-by-step execution for complex tasks.

items.headline: Private LLM
items.text: Run DeepSeek, Llama, Phi, and Gemma on-device with zero internet connectivity. Full privacy-first AI on your phone with no cloud dependency. Works with Siri shortcuts for seamless mobile integration.

items.headline: Iran Missiles Near Dimona
items.text: Strikes hit Arad and Dimona near Israel's nuclear research center, injuring 100+. First time in history a nuclear facility has been directly targeted. Major escalation in regional conflict with global implications.

items.headline: Hormuz Strait Crisis
items.text: Trump gives Iran 48hr ultimatum to reopen strait. Iran says the strait will be completely closed if the US follows through on threats. Over 5,000 vessels stranded as global shipping faces unprecedented disruption.

items.headline: Diego Garcia Attacked
items.text: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at the US-UK military base on Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean. Neither missile struck the base directly, but the attack marks a significant geographic expansion of hostilities.

items.headline: Lebanon Strikes
items.text: Israel launches new strikes targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon. The operations aim to degrade cross-border attack capabilities amid the broader regional escalation driven by the Iran crisis.

items.headline: Sudan Hospital Strike
items.text: 64 killed including 13 children in a hospital strike in western Darfur, confirmed by the WHO. The ongoing conflict continues to produce devastating civilian casualties with limited international intervention.

items.headline: Cuba Blackout
items.text: Cuba's power grid collapsed for the third time in March, leaving the entire country without electricity. The recurring infrastructure failures reflect deep systemic issues with public health and economic consequences.

items.headline: Ohio Meteor
items.text: A suspected meteor lit up skies across Ohio and Pennsylvania accompanied by sonic booms. Residents reported brilliant flashes and shaking windows. Authorities confirmed no damage but investigations continue.

items.headline: Sentiment
items.text: Bearish (Oil crisis, equities sliding)

items.headline: Superhuman AI
items.text: Zain Kahn — “Scientists use plastic to beat Parkinson’s” | Science Sunday special covering non-AI breakthroughs across space, medicine, and hardware innovation. Hubble catches comet exploding in real time. Man survives 48 hours without lungs. Edinburgh scientists convert plastic bottles to Parkinson's medication. Harvard builds chip to twist and control light. Taya AI necklace for journaling, Nosh One robot chef, iGarden Swim Jet, Fort wearable for strength training. Botox shows promise as antidepressant. Wearable compression sleeve aids cancer recovery. New cholesterol pill enlicitide cuts LDL by 60%. “The odds of witnessing a comet breaking up live through Hubble are vanishingly small”

items.headline: TAAFT
items.text: “Run LLMs on Your Phone” | Pentagon makes Palantir Maven official. White House drops national AI framework. WordPress gives AI agents publish access via MCP integration with 19 write operations. KiloClaw, LEANSpark, Unbiased Ventures pitch deck scorer, PixAI Art, ProfilePro. Karpathy says engineering is 80% delegating to AI agents. Cursor Head of Design shows designers coding actual features instead of Figma mockups. openpilot (driver assistance for 300+ cars), MetaClaw (self-evolving agent framework). “Engineering is now 80% delegating to AI agents” — Andrej Karpathy

items.headline: finstory AI
items.text: “Napkin sketch to board-ready CFO report” | Google Stitch turns hand-drawn financial sketches into premium executive reports with one prompt. Demonstrates FP&A judgment, not just formatting. One-click deployment from sketch to boardroom-ready output. The difference between generic AI output and boardroom-ready output is asking for judgment, not design. Figma stock dropped 12% on Stitch launch day. This pattern will move into every category of professional work where design meets domain expertise. “Don’t ask for a design. Ask for FP&A judgment built into a visual format.”

items.headline: Dharmesh Shah @ simple.ai
items.text: “3-Part Strategy for Keeping Up with AI” | 1) Use AI daily — don’t just study it. 2) Schedule quarterly AI opportunity reviews. 3) Try new tools with specific problems in mind, not generic curiosity. Companies that evaluate AI, find it not ready, and don’t revisit for a year fall behind competitors who were more persistent. Maintain a “not yet but soon” list. Company Research Agent, Prospect Extractor, ICP Builder on agent.ai. “The frequency with which we get new [X changes everything] values is accelerating”

items.headline: OpenAI
items.text: GPT-5.4 Superapp consolidation merges ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into one desktop app with 1.05M tokens. Targeting $25B revenue. Largest UX consolidation in AI history positions them as the default consumer AI platform.

items.headline: Google DeepMind
items.text: Gemini 3.1 Pro tops intelligence benchmarks at 57 points with 1M context and 77.1% ARC-AGI-2. Stitch disrupts the design tool category on day one. Strong multimodal reasoning across all modalities gives them unique breadth.

items.headline: Meta AI
items.text: Llama 4 open-source release focuses on agentic capability and developer ecosystem. Strategy: out-distribute rather than out-spend. Building the largest open-source AI community as a competitive moat against closed-source rivals.

items.headline: Mistral
items.text: On track for $1B ARR with the Forge enterprise platform for custom AI models. GPU-efficient architecture appeals to cost-conscious enterprises. European headquarters provides regulatory advantage as EU AI Act takes effect.

items.headline: Anthropic
items.text: Claude 4.6 leads SWE-Bench Verified at 80.8%, the top coding benchmark score. Pentagon lawsuit draws cross-industry support from OpenAI and Google employees. Enterprise deployment expanding via Microsoft Azure partnership.

items.headline: Challengers
items.text: Cursor achieves 2x commit rates with Plan Mode. MiniMax M2.5 rivals frontier models at lower cost. Xiaomi MiMo-V2-Pro enters the arena. The challenger tier is compressing the gap with incumbents faster than expected.

items.headline: Mindset
items.text: Dharmesh's 3-Part Strategy: Use AI daily, schedule quarterly opportunity reviews, adopt a problem-first tool evaluation approach. Don't dismiss AI and forget — revisit regularly with a “not yet but soon” list.

items.headline: Build
items.text: Agentic workflows without code using Gumloop, n8n, or Zapier Central. Simple research agents cost $5-20/month. Four architectures available from single-agent to multi-agent orchestration.

items.headline: Hardware
items.text: Private LLM runs DeepSeek, Llama, Phi, and Gemma on your phone with zero internet. Full privacy, no cloud dependency. Works with Siri shortcuts for hands-free private AI access.

items.headline: Creativity
items.text: Google Stitch generates high-fidelity designs from plain language. No wireframes or design background needed. Deploys in one click from sketch to production-ready output across finance, marketing, and operations.

items.headline: Productivity
items.text: Base44 Superagents run 24/7 — scan inbox, draft replies, follow up with leads, pull weekly reports. No servers or Docker needed. Always-on automation for repetitive business workflows.

items.headline: Coding
items.text: Claude Opus 4.6 leads SWE-Bench at 80.8%. Cursor Plan Mode doubles commit rates. WordPress MCP integration adds 19 write operations for AI agents to manage content programmatically.

items.headline: OpenAI Superapp — The Desktop AI Consolidation
items.text: OpenAI is merging ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into a single desktop Superapp. GPT-5.4 launched with 1M token context, scoring 75% on OSWorld-V (above the 72.4% human baseline). Revenue has surpassed $25B and the company is exploring an IPO. This marks OpenAI's pivot from model provider to platform company — a shift worth monitoring closely.

items.headline: Oil at $112/bbl — Hormuz Blockade Reshaping Global Energy
items.text: The Strait of Hormuz blockade enters week four with 3,000+ vessels stranded and Brent crude above $110/barrel — up 50% since the conflict began. 84% of Hormuz crude flows to Asia, making this an existential energy threat for the region. Trump is threatening Iranian power plants while simultaneously suggesting winding down operations. This is the dominant macro story driving every market.

items.headline: Google Stitch — Free AI Design Tool Disrupts Finance Reporting
items.text: Google launched Stitch, a free AI tool that converts plain-language prompts and napkin sketches into production-quality reports. Figma dropped 12% on the news. The finstory AI newsletter tested it on real financials: one photo produced a board-ready CFO report with KPIs, trend charts, and driver callouts — work that normally takes an FP&A analyst 3 hours. The key insight: frame prompts as judgment instructions, not formatting requests.

items.headline: ChatGPT, Codex & Atlas Merging Into Desktop Superapp
items.text: OpenAI is consolidating its three flagship products into a single unified desktop Superapp. GPT-5.4 launched with 1M token context and scored 75% on OSWorld-V, surpassing the 72.4% human baseline. Revenue exceeds $25B and the company is exploring an IPO. This signals OpenAI's ambition to own the desktop AI layer.

items.headline: Claude Sonnet 4.6 & Opus 4.6 with 1M Context
items.text: Anthropic launched Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 with 1M token context windows. Opus 4.6 is now deployed in PowerPoint and Excel for enterprise workflows. Memory features persist across conversations, and Claude Code Channels extend access to Telegram and Discord for always-on agent experiences.

items.headline: Gemini 3.1 Pro (77.1% ARC-AGI-2) + Stitch Launch
items.text: Google released Gemini 3.1 Pro scoring 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 and launched Stitch, a free AI design tool that converts descriptions into high-fidelity interfaces. Figma dropped 12% on the news. Flash-Lite arrives at $0.25/M tokens, making frontier inference accessible at commodity prices.

items.headline: Llama 4: Open-Source Agentic AI
items.text: Meta released Llama 4 with full agentic capability — the model can plan, execute multi-step tasks, and maintain context across long interactions. This is the biggest open-source AI release in years, giving developers free access to an agent-class model that can compete with proprietary alternatives.

items.headline: Nemotron 3 Super — 120B MoE at GTC
items.text: NVIDIA announced Nemotron 3 Super at GTC — a 120B-parameter hybrid MoE model with only 12B active parameters, designed for multi-agent applications in software development and cybersecurity. The architecture delivers large-model quality at small-model inference cost.

items.headline: Mistral Small 4 — 119B MoE Multimodal
items.text: Mistral released Small 4, a 119B MoE multimodal reasoning model unveiled at NVIDIA GTC alongside the Forge enterprise platform. Mistral is on track for $1B ARR, competing directly with larger labs while maintaining a lean European operation and open-weight philosophy.

items.headline: DoorDash Tasks — 8M Drivers Become AI Data Collectors
items.text: DoorDash launched Tasks, paying its 8 million Dashers to photograph menus, check shelves, and record walkthroughs for real-time business intelligence. The delivery workforce becomes the world's largest AI data collection pipeline — a novel crowdsourced approach to training AI on real-world physical environments.

items.headline: MiniMax M2.5 Rivals Claude Opus at Lower Cost
items.text: Chinese lab MiniMax released M2.5, reportedly rivaling Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 at significantly lower cost. This continues the pattern of Chinese models delivering frontier-level performance at a fraction of Western prices, pressuring the economics of the entire AI industry.

items.headline: Cursor Composer 2 — Enhanced AI Coding
items.text: Cursor released Composer 2 with enhanced AI coding capabilities, delivering frontier-level code generation at improved cost-performance ratios. The upgrade cements Cursor's position as the dominant AI-native IDE for professional developers and pressures competing coding tools.

items.headline: MIT Protein-Based Drug Design Model
items.text: MIT researchers developed a new AI model for protein-based drug design that could accelerate pharmaceutical R&D pipelines. The model enables more precise targeting of disease mechanisms through computational protein engineering, potentially reducing the time and cost of bringing new therapeutics to clinical trials.

items.headline: Iran War Week 4: Hormuz Blocked, Oil Above $110
items.text: The US-Israel conflict with Iran enters week four. The Strait of Hormuz is blocked with 3,000+ vessels stranded and oil above $110/barrel. Trump threatens to "hit and obliterate" Iranian power plants while the humanitarian toll mounts. No clear off-ramp is in sight as both sides escalate.

items.headline: Trump Suggests "Winding Down" Even as Marines Deploy
items.text: The White House is sending mixed signals — Trump publicly suggested winding down Iran operations while simultaneously approving additional Marine deployments. The contradiction underscores the lack of a coherent exit strategy as the conflict enters its fourth week with escalating costs.

items.headline: Iran Fires Ballistic Missiles at US-UK Base
items.text: Iran launched ballistic missiles at a US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean, marking a significant escalation in direct military confrontation. The strike represents one of the most direct attacks on Western military infrastructure since the conflict began, raising fears of further escalation.

items.headline: 1,000+ Killed in Lebanon Since March 2
items.text: Over 1,000 people have been killed in Lebanon since March 2, including 111 children. The humanitarian crisis deepens as the regional conflict expands beyond its initial scope, with civilian casualties mounting and international calls for a ceasefire going unanswered.

items.headline: Elon Musk Found Liable for Misleading Twitter Investors
items.text: A federal jury found Elon Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors before his $44B acquisition. One of the highest-profile securities fraud findings in Silicon Valley history, with potentially significant financial implications and questions about accountability for market manipulation.

items.headline: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director, Dies at 81
items.text: Robert Mueller, who served as FBI Director and later as Special Counsel investigating Russian interference in the 2016 election, has died at age 81. A defining figure in American law enforcement and justice, Mueller's career spanned decades of public service at the highest levels.

items.headline: Ukraine Deploys Overhead Netting Against Russian FPV Drones
items.text: Ukrainian forces are using overhead netting as a defensive countermeasure against Russian first-person-view attack drones. The low-tech solution demonstrates battlefield adaptation against increasingly sophisticated drone warfare, as both sides innovate in the evolving conflict.

items.headline: Asia Faces Existential Energy Threat from Hormuz Crisis
items.text: 84% of crude oil transiting the Strait of Hormuz flows to Asian markets, making the blockade an existential energy threat to the region. Asian economies face cascading impacts on manufacturing, transport, and inflation as the crisis enters its fourth week with no resolution in sight.

items.headline: Sentiment
items.text: Bearish (Oil shock)

items.headline: The Exoskeleton Effect
items.text: AI amplifies capability but over-reliance atrophies judgment. Use AI as a collaborator, not a replacement. Maintain critical thinking skills alongside AI tools to avoid dependency and preserve decision-making quality.

items.headline: No-Code Agentic Workflows
items.text: Gumloop enables no-code agentic workflows. n8n provides open-source automation. Claude Code + MCP servers let you build sophisticated agents without traditional coding — lowering the barrier to production-grade AI automation.

items.headline: Desktop AI & On-Device Inference
items.text: NVIDIA DGX Station brings data center AI to the desktop. Apple Private Cloud Compute runs Gemini models with strict privacy guarantees. On-device inference is becoming practical as smaller MoE models reduce compute requirements.

items.headline: Sketch-to-Production Design
items.text: Google Stitch turns sketches into production designs for free. Generative 3D and video tools are maturing rapidly. AI-assisted design is now accessible to non-designers, democratizing visual communication and reporting.

items.headline: Copilot Agent Mode
items.text: Copilot Agent Mode orchestrates cross-app tasks autonomously within Microsoft 365. Match workflow architecture to the specific use case rather than chasing maximum autonomy — the right level of AI assistance depends on the task.

items.headline: Claude Opus 4.6 + Cursor Composer 2
items.text: Claude Opus 4.6 is the current best coding model with 1M context. Cursor Composer 2 and Claude Code Channels expand AI coding accessibility. Together they represent the frontier of AI-assisted software development.
